message:
refactor(types,vdbe): move SIMD integer-record serialization from unsafe AVX2 intrinsics to safe portable_simd in fsqlite-types

The MakeRecord SIMD fast path for all-INTEGER records was implemented
directly in fsqlite-vdbe/make_record_simd.rs using raw unsafe AVX2
intrinsics, making it architecture-specific, hard to test in isolation,
and carrying unnecessary unsafe blocks.

This change:
- Adds simd_serialize_integer_record() in fsqlite-types/src/record.rs
  using nightly portable_simd (std::simd), which is safe, portable,
  and lets LLVM lower to the best available ISA automatically
- Enables #![feature(portable_simd)] on fsqlite-types
- Guts make_record_simd.rs to a thin delegating wrapper that calls the
  canonical fsqlite-types implementation
- Updates all 4 call sites in engine.rs to route through the new path

The portable_simd implementation processes 4 i64 values per iteration,
computing varint serial types and payload bytes via lane-parallel
comparisons, with a scalar tail loop for remaining columns.

Adds comprehensive tests:
- 3 deterministic unit tests for edge cases (empty, single column,
  mixed varint sizes including negative values and NULL sentinels)
- 10,000-case proptest verifying SIMD output matches scalar
  serialize_record() byte-for-byte for arbitrary integer vectors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
